cards.front: Rule for variable sharing in multithreading
cards.back: If a variable is declared in one thread and accessed in another, the compiler will generate a copy of the variable for the second thread

cards.front: Scope of a variable in multithreading
cards.back: Variables declared in a scope are accessible to all threads within that scope

cards.front: Purpose of the reduction clause in OpenMP
cards.back: To reduce data from multiple threads to a single value, reducing the complexity of the computation
A situation where the result of one iteration of a loop depends on the result of the previous iteration

cards.front: Concept of producers and consumers in multithreading
cards.back: Producers produce data, consumers consume data, and synchronization is necessary to ensure data is produced and consumed in the correct order
A cache is a small, fast memory that stores frequently-used data, reducing the time it takes to access main memory
Ensures that all threads have the same version of shared data, reducing inconsistencies and errors
A situation where multiple threads access different parts of a shared variable, resulting in wasted cache space and reduced performance
